name: Tuna Stuffed Potatoes
recipeIngredient: Fresh ground white pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash and dry the potatoes well.
2. Rub each potato with 2 tablespoons butter.
3. Bake the potatoes in a 425°F oven for 30 minutes.
4. Remove from the oven and pierce a few places with fork tines.
5. Return to the oven and continue baking until the potatoes are tender, about 30 minutes longer.
6. About 15 minutes before the potatoes are done, melt the remaining 2 tablespoons butter in a medium pan.
7. Add garlic and sauté over medium heat for 2-3 minutes.
8. Add flour and stir to form a paste.
9. Gradually stir in chicken broth and milk, stirring constantly.
10. Cook over medium heat, stirring frequently, until the mixture is thickened and smooth.
11. Stir in cheese and cook over low heat until the cheese melts.
12. Stir in chilies with any liquid and scallions.
13. Season to taste with salt and pepper.
14. Simmer, uncovered, for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
15. To serve, split each potato in half. Arrange two halves on a plate. Make a vertical slash in each potato half.
16. Fill each with about 1/4 of the tuna.
17. Spoon some or all of the cheese sauce over the top.
